title: Pilosella erratica

intro: Pilosella erratica, also known as the wandering hare's ear, is a perennial plant species belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may aid in wound healing and alleviate symptoms of gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Pilosella erratica include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects.
